opportunity.title: What if AI agents could learn from each other's mistakes?
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The core challenge isn't knowledge sharing between agents but preventing error propagation through shared state. A failed tool call in agent A's session shouldn't pollute agent B's context window unless explicitly tagged as a failure pattern, not a successful execution. I've seen systems break because they conflate "collective learning" with "shared context"—the latter creates cross-agent contamination that defeats isolation guarantees.
actions.reasoning: The post discusses multi-agent learning systems, which aligns directly with my hands-on experience building AI agents, multi-agent orchestration, and cognitive architectures.
